attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The sharp scythes on its forearms become increasingly sharp by cutting through hard objects.
It spits out a fluid that it uses to glue tree bark to its body. The fluid hardens when it touches air.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It flashes the light on its rear to communicate with other Volbeat. It loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It guides Volbeat to draw signs in the night sky. There are scholars who research the meaning of these signs.
This Pokémon came to be when the spirit of a deceased child possessed a tree stump. It now wanders the forest searching for friends.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Trevenant is very kind to Pokémon living in the forest. It doesn’t even care if these Pokémon take up residence in the greenery on its head.
If Charmander is healthy, the flame on the tip of its tail will burn vigorously and won’t go out even if it gets a bit wet.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may take a {R} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your Active {R} Pokémon.

flavorText: It is very hotheaded by nature, so it constantly seeks opponents to battle against. Its aggression will not be quelled if it doesn’t win.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 110 or less, this attack does 80 more damage.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: If you’ve been accepted by Ponyta, its burning mane is mysteriously no longer hot to the touch.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does +30 damage.

flavorText: The fastest runner becomes the leader, and it decides the herd’s pace and direction of travel.
Wavering flames similar to those of the sun appear on the surface of this Pokémon’s body.

attacks.text: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: They dwell in volcanic craters. It’s said that only a single pair of Magmortar will inhabit any given volcano.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: People call this kind of Tauros the Blaze Breed due to the hot air it snorts from its nostrils. Its three tails are intertwined.
It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack also does 20 damage for each heads to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Able to understand human speech and very intelligent, it loves to swim in the sea with people on its back.
It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Because every Prinplup considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It avoids unnecessary disputes, but it will mercilessly decimate anything that threatens the safety of its colony.

attacks.text: Take a {W} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched {W} Pokémon.

flavorText: It drifts in warm seas. It always returns to where it was born, no matter how far it may have drifted.
When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.
Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.

attacks.text: If Magmortar is on your Bench, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent and instantly sends over 20,000 volts of high-voltage electricity through them.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.

attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each point you have gotten.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When it twitches its nose, it can tell where someone is sleeping and what that person is dreaming about.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Always holding a pendulum that it swings at a steady rhythm, it causes drowsiness in anyone nearby.

attacks.text: 1 attack from among the Pokémon in your opponent's hand and deck is chosen at random, and you use the chosen attack as this attack.

flavorText: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.
Using its tail like a spring, it keeps its heart beating by bouncing constantly. If it stops, it dies.

attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 2, 4, or 6 cards in their hand, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes’ minds.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its skin is very thin. If it is exposed to light, its blood heats up, causing it to grow weak.
In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

attacks.text: Discard 2 random Energy from among the Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.
Its body is made of gas. Despite lacking substance, it can envelop an opponent of any size and cause suffocation.

attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: In total darkness, where nothing is visible, Haunter lurks, silently stalking its next victim.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Trainer cards from their hand.

abilities.text: At the end of each turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, do 20 damage to that Pokémon.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It chases people and Pokémon from its territory by causing them to experience deep, nightmarish slumbers.
This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.
The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

attacks.text: Move 2 {D} Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Hunger has disrupted this Pokémon’s hormonal balance. The Electric-type energy stored in its cheek sacs has turned into Dark-type energy.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 100 damage to itself and 50 damage to all Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside this shell is a total mystery.

attacks.text: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.
The steel section is Varoom’s actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active {M} Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It is born large to start with. It repeatedly sheds its skin as it steadily grows longer.

attacks.text: Take a {W} and a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach them to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is called the divine Pokémon. When its entire body brightens slightly, the weather changes.

attacks.text: Discard a {W} and a {L} Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can fly in spite of its big and bulky physique. It circles the globe in just 16 hours.
If you see peculiar teeth marks on boulders or trees, it means an Axew is likely living nearby.
Fraxure uses its thick tusks to neatly cut prey into two portions—one to eat now and one to save for later.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

flavorText: It overwhelms enemies with its prized tusks, which are sharp enough to cut through a metal transmission tower in one strike.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.
If it inflates to sing a lullaby, it can perform longer and cause sure drowsiness in its audience.
The rich, fluffy fur that covers its body feels so good that anyone who feels it can’t stop touching it.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.

attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon like things neat and tidy. They are always sweeping and dusting their habitat, using their tails as brooms.
The oil that seeps from its body is really smooth. For people troubled by bad skin, this oil is an effective treatment.

abilities.text: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

trainer.text: Your opponent discards cards from their hand until they have 4 cards in their hand.
Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. Draw a card. If you can't shuffle in 2 cards, you can't use this card.
During this turn, if your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack used by your Haxorus, you get 1 more point.
Draw a card for each Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play (both yours and your opponent's).
At the end of each player's turn, that player draws cards until they have 3 cards in their hand.

attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon ex that has any Energy attached. If you do, discard a random Energy from that Pokémon.

flavorText: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

trainer.text:
If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.

The {M} Pokémon this card is attached to takes −50 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
